title: Hydronic Pumps
full_text:
8.4.4.14 Hydronic Pumps
Hydronic Pumps
1) Except as provided in Sentences (2) and (3), each hydronic pump of the reference building shall have a total static head and efficiency identical to that of the corresponding pump of the proposed building.
2) Where the proposed building uses more than one pump in a given hydronic system, the peak shaft power demand of the reference building's pump shall be modeled as being identical to the combined peak shaft power demand of the proposed building's pumps. (See Note A-8.4.4.14.(2).)
3) Where the total static head or efficiency of the proposed building's hydronic pump is not known, the characteristics of the reference building's hydronic pump shall be based on the peak power demand, in W/(L/s), of the proposed building's pump.
4) Pump power, P, versus flow rate, V, shall be calculated using one of the following equations: [Equation: \begin{cases} P_{partload} = P_{rated} \times E & \text{if } \frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}} < D \\ P_{partload} = P_{rated} \times \left[A + B \times \left(\frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}}\right) + C \times \left(\frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}}\right)^{2}\right] & \text{if } \frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}} \geq D \end{cases}] where Vpartload = flow rate at part-load conditions, in L/s, Vrated = flow rate at rated conditions, in L/s, Ppartload = power draw at part-load conditions, in kW, Prated = power draw at rated conditions, in kW, and A, B, C, D, E = coefficients defined in Table 8.4.4.14. Table 8.4.4.14. Coefficients Used in Calculating Pump Power versus Flow Rate Forming Part of Sentence 8.4.4.14.(4) Coefficients Type of Pump Pump Riding its Curve Pump with Variable Speed Drive 0.227143 1.178929 –0.41071 0.47 0.68 0.00153028 0.00520806 1.0086242 0.20 0.04
5) Variable-flow pumps shall be modeled as a pump riding its curve.
6) Where the secondary system defined in Tables 8.4.4.7.-A and 8.4.4.13. represents equipment meeting the requirements of Article 5.2.12.1. and whose minimum performance includes pump energy (in part or in whole), the performance of the heating and cooling equipment of the secondary system shall be adjusted so that the maximum power demand of the system, including pump power demand, meets the requirements of Article 5.2.12.1.

sentences.text: Except as provided in Sentences (2) and (3), each hydronic pump of the reference building shall have a total static head and efficiency identical to that of the corresponding pump of the proposed building.

Where the proposed building uses more than one pump in a given hydronic system, the peak shaft power demand of the reference building's pump shall be modeled as being identical to the combined peak shaft power demand of the proposed building's pumps. (See Note A-8.4.4.14.(2).)

Where the total static head or efficiency of the proposed building's hydronic pump is not known, the characteristics of the reference building's hydronic pump shall be based on the peak power demand, in W/(L/s), of the proposed building's pump.

Pump power, P, versus flow rate, V, shall be calculated using one of the following equations: [Equation: \begin{cases} P_{partload} = P_{rated} \times E & \text{if } \frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}} < D \\ P_{partload} = P_{rated} \times \left[A + B \times \left(\frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}}\right) + C \times \left(\frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}}\right)^{2}\right] & \text{if } \frac{V_{partload}}{V_{rated}} \geq D \end{cases}] where Vpartload = flow rate at part-load conditions, in L/s, Vrated = flow rate at rated conditions, in L/s, Ppartload = power draw at part-load conditions, in kW, Prated = power draw at rated conditions, in kW, and A, B, C, D, E = coefficients defined in Table 8.4.4.14. Table 8.4.4.14. Coefficients Used in Calculating Pump Power versus Flow Rate Forming Part of Sentence 8.4.4.14.(4) Coefficients Type of Pump Pump Riding its Curve Pump with Variable Speed Drive 0.227143 1.178929 –0.41071 0.47 0.68 0.00153028 0.00520806 1.0086242 0.20 0.04
Variable-flow pumps shall be modeled as a pump riding its curve.

Where the secondary system defined in Tables 8.4.4.7.-A and 8.4.4.13. represents equipment meeting the requirements of Article 5.2.12.1. and whose minimum performance includes pump energy (in part or in whole), the performance of the heating and cooling equipment of the secondary system shall be adjusted so that the maximum power demand of the system, including pump power demand, meets the requirements of Article 5.2.12.1.
